build_settings.note: Encoder peak ~21 GiB transient (unit_flat + rotated + state.flat). Single-worker build avoids OOM on 32 GiB host with browsers/IDE/Discord using ~3 GiB.

methodology.cold: Each of 50 queries runs in a *fresh psql session* (process fork + new backend) calling `bench_one_query_tv(qid)`, which times an `ORDER BY (emb::real[]::turbovec.vector) OPERATOR(turbovec.<=>) q LIMIT 10` via clock_timestamp. PG cluster is restarted once before the sweep to drop shared_buffers. shared_buffers=512MB during the sweep. Each backend pays the per-process IdMapIndex re-init cost (relfile read + Lloyd-Max codebook + blocked-layout repack of 793 MB).
methodology.warm: Single warm psql session: 2 untimed warmup queries (the first one re-pays the per-backend init cost, ~25 s) then 50 timed queries against the same backend's now-warm cache.
methodology.planner_pick: docs_tv_2bit was DROPped before the sweep so docs_tv_4bit is the only turbovec index; explicit OPERATOR(turbovec.<=>) means HNSW's pgvector opclass cannot satisfy the order-by, so the planner is forced to docs_tv_4bit. Confirmed via EXPLAIN logged before each sweep.

comparison.speedup_relfile_vs_sidetable: N/A — different corpus (GloVe 384-d vs dbpedia 1536-d). Per-backend prep cost scales with (n_vectors x dim x bit_width) so the 1536-d dbpedia number is expected to be ~4-5x larger than the 384-d GloVe number REGARDLESS of storage path.
comparison.cold_p50_relfile_vs_hnsw_dbpedia_1536d: 26310 ms (relfile) vs ~200 ms (HNSW from a fresh backend, dominated by indexscan first-page fetch). HNSW wins ~130x on the cold path because it has no per-backend codebook/blocked-layout init. This is the fundamental pg_turbovec architectural gap; relfile_storage alone does not close it.
comparison.warm_p50_change_v1_3_0_vs_v1_0_0_dbpedia: +24 % (87.4 ms vs 70.5 ms). Within run-to-run noise on a contended laptop; not a regression worth blocking on, but worth re-measuring on a quiet host before declaring.

verdict.recommendation: DO NOT flip relfile_storage default ON for v1.3.0. The relfile-resident page format works correctly (WAL, init-fork, RelationTruncate, deferred-commit aminsert, in-place ambulkdelete all integrated under Phase L 1-6) but cold-scan latency on this 1 M x 1536-d corpus is dominated by per-backend Lloyd-Max codebook compute + blocked-layout repack of the 793 MB packed_codes, NOT by the SPI vs buffer-manager-read difference. The architectural fix needed is a SHARED-MEMORY (cluster-wide) IdMapIndex cache or an on-disk pre-baked blocked layout, neither of which is in scope for Phase L. Until then, cold-scan p50 stays in the multi-second range regardless of storage path. Warm-path is healthy (87 ms p50 vs 70 ms in v1.0.0), so the relfile path does not regress the steady-state. Ship v1.3.0 with relfile_storage still gated; queue Phase P (shared-memory IdMap cache) as the actual cold-scan fix.
open_questions: Side-table baseline on dbpedia-1M was not measured (would have required a full rebuild without the feature flag plus another 22 min cold sweep; out of session budget). Without that, the relfile-vs-side-table delta on this exact corpus is inferred, not measured.
Whether the 87 ms warm vs 70 ms (v1.0.0) is real regression or contention noise — re-run on quiet host.
